PolicyEffect.description:
The verdict a policy rule (or the default posture) yields.

Ordered by restrictiveness for incomparable-rule tie-breaking: `Deny` is a hard override (handled separately), and among non-deny verdicts `RequireReview` is more restrictive than `Allow`.

oneOf.description: Permit only after human review. The safe default posture.
Refuse the action. A hard override — no `allow` overturns it.

PolicyFreezeEntry.description: One principal's freeze/unfreeze record inside a [`PolicyFreezeOutput`].

effect.description: The recorded effect (`deny` for a freeze, `allow` for an unfreeze).

principal.description: The principal whose actions were frozen/unfrozen.

PolicyPrincipal.description:
Who is attempting an action.

`agent` is a non-human caller (an AI harness authoring, applying, or remediating). `human` is a person. In v0 the principal is supplied explicitly (`rocky policy check --principal …`); auto-detection is a later phase.

description:
JSON output for `rocky policy freeze` / `rocky policy unfreeze` — the kill switch.

A freeze records a decision entry per matched principal into the existing policy-decision ledger; at the enforcement seam an active freeze forces `deny`. `unfreeze` records a superseding entry that lifts it. No config file is rewritten and no new table is created.

lifted.description: `true` when this call lifted a freeze rather than establishing one.

notes.description: Advisory notes about enforcement status — e.g. a warning that the freeze was recorded but is inert because the project has no `[policy]` block. Empty when the freeze is enforceable.

recorded_at.description: RFC 3339 wall clock when the freeze/unfreeze was recorded.

scope.description: The scope selector the freeze targets (`any`, `layer=…`, `tag=…`, …).

title: PolicyFreezeOutput
